title: salvia barrelieri subsp. bicolor
intro: Salvia barrelieri subsp. bicolor, also known as the Sicilian sage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family, characterized by its distinctive bicolor flowers, which are typically blue and white in color. The medicinal properties of Salvia barrelieri subsp. bicolor include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to improve cognitive function, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve heart health. In herbal preparations, Salvia barrelieri subsp. bicolor is often used as a tea or tincture, and can also be dried and used as an herbal infusion.
